name: Carey Poverello
description_short: Carey Poverello Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, non-profit credit union serving five Ohio counties since 1950, offering savings accounts, loans, and nationwide shared branching access.
description_long:
Carey Poverello Federal Credit Union was established in 1950 and operates as a non-profit, member-owned financial institution regulated by the NCUA. Based in Carey, Ohio, it serves a defined community field of membership across Wyandot, Seneca, Hancock, Hardin, and Crawford counties, plus immediate family members of existing members and employees. The institution is governed by a volunteer board of directors elected by members to three-year terms, with Austin Pahl serving as CEO.

The credit union offers a range of consumer financial services including savings accounts, checking accounts, money market accounts, debit cards, vehicle loans, boat and camper financing, and general-purpose loans. Members can access online banking services and utilize a loan calculator for planning purposes. The institution provides check reordering services and notary services at its main branch located at 201 North Vance Street in Carey, Ohio. Debit card fraud reporting is available through dedicated phone lines (800-383-8000 for lost/stolen cards, 855-219-5399 for fraud).

What distinguishes Carey Poverello is its participation in the CO-OP Shared Branching network, which connects it with more than 5,000 credit unions nationwide. This allows members to conduct transactions at shared branch locations coast to coast, providing convenience for traveling members or those relocating. As a non-profit credit union, the institution emphasizes lower fees and loan rates compared to traditional for-profit banks, with profits reinvested for member benefit.

Carey Poverello is best suited for individuals within its membership field who value community-focused banking and personalized service. The primary limitation is its restricted membership eligibility based on geography and employment, which excludes those outside its five-county service area unless they have family connections to current members. Operating hours are limited to weekday mornings through early evenings and Saturday mornings only.
services: CO-OP shared branching at 5,000+ locations nationwide
pros: Access to 5,000+ shared branch locations nationwide through CO-OP network for member convenience
Non-profit structure typically results in lower fees and competitive loan rates compared to traditional banks
Community charter membership open to anyone living, working, or volunteering in five Ohio counties
Volunteer board of directors composed of members ensures member-focused governance
Serving the community since 1950 with established local roots and stability
Online banking portal and digital services available for remote access
Specialized financing programs for vehicles, boats, and campers with dedicated loan application process
cons: Restricted membership field limits access to those outside five Ohio counties unless related to existing members
Limited physical branch network with single main location; relies on CO-OP shared branching for expansion
Saturday hours only 9:00am-12:00pm and no Sunday operations, limiting weekend accessibility
Smaller institution with fewer resources for technology innovation compared to regional/national banks
Drive-through closes at 4:30pm weekdays and noon Saturday, creating limited evening access options
best_for: Residents and workers in Wyandot, Seneca, Hancock, Hardin, and Crawford counties seeking community-based banking
Families with existing credit union members who qualify through immediate family relationship eligibility
Consumers prioritizing lower fees and loan rates through non-profit financial institution membership
Individuals needing vehicle, boat, or recreational equipment financing with personalized service
diagnosis: Carey Poverello Federal Credit Union is ideal for residents in its five-county Ohio service area who want community-focused banking with lower fees and rates through a member-owned non-profit structure. The main caveat is membership eligibility—those outside the geographic field must have existing family connections to members, making it inaccessible for most consumers outside the defined service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Carey Poverello — NCUA-insured credit union in Carey, OH. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
